message:
🐛 fix(ui): add dark-mode support flagged by Auto-QA (Issue 9071) (#9210)

Address Auto-QA UI Design pass findings on ~14 files using light-only
color classes or inline data-driven colors that won't auto-switch with
the active theme.

Principle: prefer semantic tokens (bg-muted, text-muted-foreground,
border-border, bg-muted/30) over `dark:` duplication so the neutral/
default surfaces auto-adapt via CSS variables.

Changes by file:
- DynamicCard, VClusterStatus, KagentiAgentFleet, openkruise_status,
  ProwCIMonitor: swap `bg-gray-500/*` fallback badges to `bg-muted`
  (+ `border-border` where applicable) so the default/unknown status
  adapts to both themes.
- KagentStatusCard, KagentiStatusCard: swap `bg-white/5` surface tint on
  metric tiles + progress tracks to `bg-muted/30` for theme-adapting
  subtle tint.
- Missions: `pending` cluster-status row uses `bg-muted` +
  `bg-muted-foreground` for the bar; non-pending states keep their
  tinted accent colors which read on both themes.
- Game2048, Solitaire: comment-only — white-on-gradient/yellow-overlay
  art is theme-independent by design (games have their own visual
  identity), not candidates for `dark:` variants.
- WorkflowMatrix: comment-only — heatmap cells use stepped gray-500
  opacities (40/50/60) to encode cancelled/skipped/neutral/stale; gray-
  500 is mid-gray and reads on both themes, opacity stepping must
  remain stable.
- llmd/HardwareLeaderboard, llmd/LLMdFlow, llmd/PDDisaggregation:
  comment-only — inline `background`/`color`/`textShadow` are
  data-driven accent colors (CONFIG_COLORS, loadColors.glow,
  metricConfig[metric].color) and intentionally not theme-switched.

All `Issue 9071` references in source comments use the word form (not
`#9071`) to avoid the `ui-ux-standard` hex-ratchet in multi-line JSX
comments.

Fixes #9071

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch: @@ -176,6 +176,11 @@ export function HardwareLeaderboard() { {row.hardware} {row.model} + {/* Issue 9071: inline `background`/`color` are data-driven accent + * colors from CONFIG_COLORS (per-configuration brand color). + * These are intentionally not theme-switched — the /20 alpha + * background + full-saturation foreground pairing reads on + * both light and dark by design. */}

@@ -5,6 +5,13 @@
  * glowing gauges, time-series sparklines, and interactive elements.
  *
  * Now supports live data from selected llm-d stack via StackContext.
+ *
+ * Issue 9071 (dark-mode pass): inline `style={{ backgroundColor|color|textShadow }}`
+ * uses in this file are intentionally data-driven — colors come from
+ * `loadColors.glow`, `COLORS.{prefill|decode|kv-transfer}`, and
+ * `metricConfig[metric].color` (per-metric brand color). These are accent
+ * colors, not surface chrome, and are designed to read on both light and
+ * dark backgrounds. They are not candidates for `dark:` variants.
  */
 import { useState, useEffect, useMemo, useRef } from 'react'
 import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion'

@@ -5,6 +5,12 @@
  * with animated token transfer between stages.
  *
  * Uses live stack data when available, demo data when in demo mode.
+ *
+ * Issue 9071 (dark-mode pass): inline `style={{ backgroundColor }}` uses in
+ * this file are data-driven load-indicator colors (amber `#f59e0b` when
+ * `server.load > 70`, otherwise the prefill/decode brand `color`). These are
+ * accent indicators that read on both light and dark backgrounds — not
+ * candidates for `dark:` variants.
  */
 import { useState, useEffect, useMemo } from 'react'
 import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion'

@@ -33,6 +33,11 @@ const SPARSE_WORKFLOW_MIN_CELLS = 2
  * satisfy the ui-ux-standard ratchet and make a future i18n pass easy. */
 const LABEL_FILTER_REPO = 'Filter by repo'
 
+// Issue 9071: Auto-QA flagged `bg-gray-500/*` cells as light-only. These are
+// heatmap cells where the distinct opacity levels (40/50/60) encode the
+// cancelled/skipped/neutral/stale states and must stay visually distinguishable
+// on both themes. gray-500 is mid-gray and reads on both light and dark
+// backgrounds, so no theme-switching is applied.
 const CELL_CLASS: Record<string, string> = {
   success: 'bg-green-500/70 hover:bg-green-500',
   failure: 'bg-red-500/80 hover:bg-red-500',
